source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A long wooden plank is moving to the right with a constant velocity $v_{0}$ on a smooth horizontal surface. A small slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank with no initial velocity. The masses of the slider and the plank are $m$ and $2m$, respectively, and the coefficient of kinetic friction between them is $\mu$. The acceleration due to gravity is $g$.

1. Determine the magnitude of their common velocity when the slider is stationary relative to the plank.
2. At a certain moment, the velocity of the plank is twice the velocity of the slider. Determine the distance from the slider to the rightmost end of the plank at this moment.
3. If a horizontal external force to the right is applied to the plank at the same time as the slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank, such that the plank maintains uniform linear motion until the slider comes to rest relative to the plank, determine the time of motion of the slider during this process.
4. If a horizontal external force to the right is applied to the plank at the same time as the slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank, such that the plank maintains uniform linear motion until the slider comes to rest relative to the plank, calculate the work done by the external force during this process.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false suggest some intended reliance on a diagram
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 valid image asset with registry_passed=true

However, the full mechanics setup, variables, and all four sub-questions are explicitly stated in question_text, with no direct reference such as 'as shown in the figure', so image reliance is not scored as very high
multi_step: The problem has 4 linked sub-questions rather than a single direct query
Solving requires combining multiple physics relations: friction, relative motion, momentum/common velocity, kinematics, and work
Part 2 introduces an intermediate condition ('plank velocity is twice slider velocity'), which adds conditional multi-step reasoning
Part 3 and Part 4 add a modified setup with an external force, requiring transfer of earlier reasoning to a new case
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text provides explicit closed-form expressions for all four requested outputs
Each requested quantity is a concrete symbolic result (velocity, distance, time, work), which is amenable to algebraic or rule-based checking
Verification may still require symbolic-equivalence handling because mathematically equivalent expressions could be written in different forms

decision_reasons: Asset coverage is complete: question, answer, and a valid image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded issues.
The sample has strong reasoning value because the multi_step_score is very high (0.93) and the task contains multiple linked physics sub-questions.
The sample is highly verifiable (verifiability_score=0.9) since the answer provides explicit symbolic results for the requested quantities.
Recorded risks are manageable rather than disqualifying: subpart alignment and symbolic-equivalence checking add evaluation complexity, but core semantics are intact and recoverable.

If a horizontal external force to the right is applied to the plank at the same time as the slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank, such that the plank maintains uniform linear motion until the slider comes to rest relative to the plank, calculate the work done by the external force during this process.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
A long wooden plank is moving to the right with a constant velocity $v_{0}$ on a smooth horizontal surface. A small slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank with no initial velocity. The masses of the slider and the plank are $m$ and $2m$, respectively, and the coefficient of kinetic friction between them is $\mu$. The acceleration due to gravity is $g$.

1. Determine the magnitude of their common velocity when the slider is stationary relative to the plank.
2. At a certain moment, the velocity of the plank is twice the velocity of the slider. Determine the distance from the slider to the rightmost end of the plank at this moment.
3. If a horizontal external force to the right is applied to the plank at the same time as the slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank, such that the plank maintains uniform linear motion until the slider comes to rest relative to the plank, determine the time of motion of the slider during this process.
4. If a horizontal external force to the right is applied to the plank at the same time as the slider is gently placed on the rightmost end of the plank, such that the plank maintains uniform linear motion until the slider comes to rest relative to the plank, calculate the work done by the external force during this process.
